rulings.Case Title: PAUL D. LAPACHET ET AL VS. LARON SANDERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Restrict Public Access To The Record Per California Rule Of Court 2.550(D)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 11, 2025, line 1. Plaintiff's Motion for Order to Restrict Public Access to the Record is DENIED. First, the motion is premature because Defendant need not vacate the unit until December 2025. Plaintiff warns of the consequences of an ejectment judgment, yet there is no judgment in this case. Second, Plaintiff does not explain how it is narrowly tailored to seal the entire record of a case. Finally, the Court cannot make the required factual findings under CRC 2.550 because the only declaration is from counsel, who does not lay the foundation for his assertions regarding tenant screenings, background checks, Defendant's economic opportunities, and more. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GARY M WAITE VS. TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 11, 2025, line 2. Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Judicially noticeable facts show that on May 8, 2023, Plaintiffs signed a contract in Pro Per waiving their rights to pursue this lawsuit against Defendant under California Civil Code Section 1542. In its late-filed Opposition, Plaintiff's only argument pertaining to this waiver is that other lawsuits are ongoing, with no explanation as to what these lawsuits are or how their pendency affects the validity Plaintiffs' waiver that occurred on May 8, 2023. To the extent that Plaintiffs' Opposition claims that attorney Mr. Higginbotham's conduct affects the validity of their Section 1542 waiver, the Court notes that Plaintiff signed the waiver in Pro Per and represented that they were advised of the importance of seeking legal advice on the contract's terms. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GAETANI REAL ESTATE VS. ARCHIE CHOICE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continue Trial Date
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 11, 2025, line 4. Defendant's Motion to Continue Trial Date is GRANTED, per the stipulation of the parties. rulings.Case Title: THOMAS PAINE SQUARE LLC VS. PAULETTE JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 11, 2025, line 5. Defendant's Motion to Vacate Default Judgment is GRANTED. The default of defendant Paulette Johnson is set aside. The judgment entered on July 14, 2025 is vacated. The writ issued on July 28, 2025 is recalled. The Court notes plaintiff filed substantive opposition on the merits of this motion, and the declaration referenced in plaintiff's opposition is not on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: UNITY HOMES, INC. VS. SEQUOIA GANT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for August 11, 2025, line 7. Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The 30-Day Notice fails to advise the tenant of their right to defend the action in court, which is expressly required by lease. By this action, plaintiff seeks to enforce defendant's obligations under the lease agreement. In doing so, plaintiff must comply with their own obligations under the same agreement. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
